The tracing most likely shows a sinusoidal pattern – a smooth, regular, low‑amplitude waveform that rises and falls in a sinus‑like fashion.
This pattern is classic for fetal anemia.
In a healthy, primigravida with no prior pregnancies, the most common cause of fetal anemia is parvovirus B19 infection (which can cause aplastic crisis in the fetus).
Therefore, the most likely cause of the observed fetal heart rate pattern is parvovirus B19 infection leading to fetal anemia.
